business.headline: Sensex falls 500 points: 3 reasons why stock market is falling todayMarkets opened sharply lower on Monday as rising Middle East tensions, higher crude prices and global weakness hit sentiment. The slide sharpened concerns over inflation, foreign outflows and pressure on technology stocks.

Stock market falls amid fresh Iran-Israel flare up, crude oil climbs above $96The selloff was broad-based, with almost all major stocks trading in the red. Investors turned cautious after fresh conflict in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher and raised concerns about inflation and global economic growth.
Stock markets likely to fall today: 3 reasons whyGIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,138 at 8:06 am IST, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open about 1% lower than its previous close of 23,366.70.

Crude oil rises over 3%. Is $100 a barrel back in sight?Brent crude rose 3.45% to $96.30 per barrel, while US benchmark WTI crude climbed 3.41% to $93.63 per barrel during trading as of 8:10 am. The sharp rebound erased most of Friday's losses, when oil prices had fallen on hopes that diplomatic efforts could ease tensions in the region.

Your flat is delayed. Your EMI isn't. What now?You're paying the EMI. The builder missed the deadline. With over 5 lakh homes stuck across India, delayed possession has become a financial nightmare for thousands of buyers. Here's what experts say you can do when your dream home doesn't arrive on time.

Rosneft CEO predicts India will drive 50% of global oil demand growth by 2035Rosneft CEO cited International Energy Agency estimates projecting that India's oil consumption would reach nearly eight million barrels per day by 2035, representing growth of 44 per cent, while global demand overall would increase by about five per cent.

Indians have no will for a WILLA house. A few bank accounts. Some investments. Perhaps a family business. For many Indians, these assets represent decades of hard work. Yet surprisingly few leave behind clear instructions on who gets what, creating uncertainty for the very people they wanted to protect.

Oil India reports second gas discovery in Andaman offshore blockOil India has reported natural gas in the Vijayapuram-3 exploratory well in the Andaman offshore basin. The find adds to evidence of hydrocarbon potential in the block as appraisal work continues.

India-US trade deal's first tranche likely by mid-July, says Piyush GoyalSpeaking about the discussions, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said the meetings held between June 2 and June 4 were very encouraging.

India's economy grows 7.7% in FY26 despite global turmoil, war concernsProvisional government estimates show economic growth accelerated from the previous year, with robust expansion in the January-March quarter helping lift full-year GDP growth to 7.7%.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as RBI holds rates; rupee logs biggest gain since AprilMarkets ended lower after the RBI kept the repo rate at 5.25%, while the rupee posted its biggest one-day gain since April. Investors are now weighing inflation, crude prices, foreign outflows and West Asia tensions for clues on market direction.

Gold, FDs or real estate? I asked experts where to invest during rupee weaknessWhen the rupee comes under pressure, many investors start looking for a safe haven. But which asset actually delivers—gold, FDs or real estate? I spoke to experts to find out.

Before Sebi stepped in, Rajesh Exports had already raised eyebrows for yearsSebi's interim order against Rajesh Exports has brought fresh scrutiny to its reported revenues and subsidiary disclosures. The action has revived years of public questions over how the company matched vast turnover with thin profits.

The hidden inflation nobody is talking about: Transport inflationAs transportation costs increase across the supply chain, businesses eventually pass those higher expenses on to consumers. The result is 'invisible inflation' – a force that pushes up the cost of almost everything but is rarely talked about on its own.

RBI MPC meeting: 7 key takeaways on repo rate, inflation, growth and moreThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has announced its latest monetary policy decisions, keeping the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% and maintaining a neutral stance. Here are seven key takeaways from the RBI's June MPC meeting.

Rajesh Exports shares hit 5% lower circuit for 2nd straight day after Sebi orderRajesh Exports shares hit the 5% lower circuit for a second straight session after Sebi's interim order. The sell-off reflects investor caution as the regulator examines alleged revenue inflation and orders a fresh forensic audit.

RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%: What it means for your home loan and EMIFor millions of home loan borrowers, the RBI's repo rate decision is closely watched because it can influence borrowing costs. With the central bank keeping the repo rate steady at 5.25%, should you expect any change in your monthly EMI?

RBI rolls out multiple measures to attract more dollars amid rupee weaknessThe RBI has unveiled a package of bond, swap and deposit measures to draw more dollars into India while keeping the repo rate unchanged. The steps are aimed at easing foreign currency liquidity strains as oil prices rise and investor outflows weigh on the rupee.

Higher crude prices forcing a rethink on inflation and growth outlook, says RBIThe RBI kept the repo rate unchanged as Sanjay Malhotra warned that the West Asia conflict is lifting oil prices and disrupting supplies. The central bank has cut its growth forecast and raised its inflation outlook as cost pressures begin to build.

Centre exempts foreign investors from capital gains tax on govt bondsThe tax break is aimed at boosting foreign investment in government bonds, improving post-tax returns for overseas investors and helping attract more stable capital flows into India.

entertainment.headline: Amid Peddi row, Sanjay Gupta says social media is destroying young actors' careers Sanjay Gupta reacted indirectly to the trolling over controversial scenes in Ram Charan's Peddi. He said social media is becoming a ruthless force for actors whose careers have only just begun.

Belmont Cameli brings his Off Campus moves to Kid LAROI concert and fans lose it Belmont Cameli surprised fans at The Kid LAROI's Los Angeles concert by breaking out some familiar moves. The viral moment has Off Campus fans revisiting one scene in particular.

Hai Jawani Toh... box office: Weekend boost takes Varun's film to Rs 23 crore Varun Dhawan and David Dhawan's Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai has held steady through its opening weekend. The film stayed ahead despite multiple releases, taking its worldwide gross to Rs 37.30 crore.

Bandar box office Day 3: Bobby Deol's film misses opening weekend rush Bobby Deol's Bandar posed a steady Sunday and took its India nett to Rs 2.45 crore. The film has drawn praise for its storytelling and Deol's performance despite a modest theatrical run.

What's wrong with Aamir marrying at 60? Rakhee Gulzar questions ageist scrutiny Rakhee Gulzar has welcomed Aamir Khan's decision to marry Gauri Spratt on July 5, 2026. She said love should not be defined by age.

All bout Jennifer Winget's wedding with Singapore businessman Ishmael Wedding bells may be on the horizon for actor Jennifer Winget. Fresh details about her relationship with businessman William Ishmael and their reported plans have emerged, giving fans plenty to talk about.

Tony Awards 2025 winners: Ragtime, Death of a Salesman and Schmigadoon dominate The 79th Tony Awards honoured Broadway's top productions and performances at Radio City Music Hall. Ragtime, Arthur Miller's Death of a Salesman, Schmigadoon emerged top victors across several categories.

Peddi box office day 4: Ram Charan film wraps up opening weekend with winning run Actors Ram Charan and Janhvi Kapoor's Peddi continues its strong box office run with a Day 4 boost. The film has crossed a major global milestone.

Explained: Why Nicolas Cage's Spider-Noir is distinct from Tom Holland's Spider-Man Moody noir and superhero thrills have made Spider-Noir a global rage. In India, many fans of Tom Holland's Spider-Man era are yet to notice the phenomenal show.

Lewis Hamilton's Monaco kiss for Kim Kardashian steals the spotlight Lewis Hamilton and Kim Kardashian turned heads at the Monaco Grand Prix after a public display of affection sparked fresh buzz. The Ferrari star later had plenty to say about her support.

Screams, tears and hugs: Taylor Lautner's baby reveal moment is pure joy. Watch Taylor Lautner's reaction to learning the gender of his baby is stealing the spotlight. The actor and wife Tay Lautner shared their emotional gender reveal, but it was one heartfelt confession afterwards that had fans talking.

Actor Salim Kumar's son Chandu asks paparazzi to back off at father's funeral Malayalam actor Salim Kumar was cremated with State honours at his residence in Ernakulam on Sunday. During the funeral, his son Chandu asked paparazzi to back off as the family performed the last rites.

AR Rahman honours BSF personnel with special performance at Attari Border. Watch AR Rahman performed at the Attari Border on June 7 during Jai Ho – A Tribute to the Bravehearts, a special event honouring BSF personnel. Organised by the team of Main Vaapas Aaunga, the programme also featured songs from the upcoming Imtiaz Ali film.

Kangana Ranaut highlights sexualisation of nurses, calls for greater respect Kangana Ranaut said nurses are "the most sexualised" and deserve more respect for their contribution to healthcare. The actor also highlighted the challenges faced by the profession, including long hours, low pay and a lack of recognition.

Aamir Khan to celebrate Lagaan's silver jubilee with grand reunion: Report Aamir Khan is reportedly hosting a grand reunion for the cast and crew of Lagaan as the Oscar-nominated film completes 25 years.
Janhvi Kapoor's Peddi row: Nithya Menen urges actors to set boundaries on screen Actor Nithya Menen joined the Peddi debate, backing Janhvi Kapoor amid criticism over the film's portrayal of women.

Steven Spielberg cheers Obsession, Backrooms for low-budget wins Steven Spielberg has praised the box-office success of indie horror films Obsession and Backrooms. He gave a shout-out to the young filmmakers for achieving impressive results.

Varun Dhawan on Salman Khan's late-night unfiltered posts: He's in another zone Varun Dhawan said Salman Khan's late-night social media posts reflect his authentic personality. He said fame can be lonely for actors and stressed staying true to oneself.

Imtiaz Ali recalls being abducted from Delhi college hostel over political dispute Imtiaz Ali recalled a shocking incident from his college days when he was abducted from his Delhi hostel after a dispute over a political poster.

general news.headline: TMC's Jahangir 'Pushpa' Khan arrested near Nepal border after weeks in hidingJahangir, who had adopted the moniker 'Pushpa' as a challenge to police observer Ajay Pal Sharma, known as 'Singham', was arrested by the Bengal STF near the Nepal border after weeks on the run.

Vijay's TVK not invited to today's INDIA bloc meet, Congress explains why  Live UpdatesAccording to sources, there is still no clarity on whether TVK will attend the meeting at Delhi's Constitution Club, and a decision on inviting actor-turned-politician Vijay's party is likely to be taken later today. However, the Congress, TVK's ally in Tamil Nadu, said that parties without MPs are not invited to attend the meeting.

Thank you, darling: Trump rips off mic, walks out of heated interviewDonald Trump walked out of NBC News' Meet the Press interview after clashing with Kristen Welker over 2020 presidential election fraud claims and California vote counting.

NEET re-exam: Paper setters under lockdown till June 21 amid tight securityAhead of the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination, paper setters, moderators and translators have been moved to a secure undisclosed facility under lockdown. The extraordinary step reflects efforts to prevent another leak scare and rebuild trust after the cancelled May exam.

Sonam Wangchuk from Ladakh. The lion, not the cockroachMany know Sonam Wangchuk, the climate activist and educationist, who backed the Cockroach Janta Party protest at Jantar Mantar. But not many know the other Sonam Wangchuk, who's also from Ladakh. Colonel Sonam Wangchuk, the 'Lion of Ladakh', fought in the icy heights during the 1999 Kargil War.

Massive 7.8 earthquake kills 4 in Philippines; tsunami waves up to 4-ft recordedA powerful earthquake off Mindanao sent residents rushing to safety, knocked out power and communications, and triggered tsunami alerts across parts of Asia. Authorities fear aftershocks and damage assessments are still underway.

Israeli missiles hit Iran in retaliatory strike, 1st such attacks since April truce Live updatesIsrael said it had struck military targets in Iran after loud explosions were heard across Tehran and several other cities. The attacks came as Tehran and Tel Aviv traded threats, and despite US President Donald Trump urging Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu not to retaliate against Iranian strikes, insisting that he "calls the shots."

A hidden Himalayan toy train route comes alive. It can match Kalka-Shimla magicThe Pathankot-Jogindernagar toy train service has resumed years after flash floods destroyed parts of the tracks in 2022. Overshadowed by the Kalka-Shimla Railway, its fewer crowds, uninterrupted mountain views and the "bliss of solitude" make a strong case for calling it India's most underrated rail journey.

Video: Frantic chase as ground equipment hits 3 planes at Delhi airport amid stormWhile Air India has not officially issued a statement on the incident, an airline source said along with three Air India planes, aircraft belonging to other operators were also affected by the hostile weather conditions.

Live: Iran says Strait of Hormuz will stay open but ships may face transit feesIsrael-Iran War Live Updates: The Israel-Iran conflict has entered a dangerous new phase, with both sides exchanging attacks across multiple fronts. Israel says it carried out fresh strikes on military targets in western and central Iran, while explosions were reported in Tehran, Tabriz and Isfahan. Iran has reportedly fired missiles at Israel for the first time since the April cease-fire, signalling a sharp escalation in hostilities. The tensions have also spilled beyond the two countries, with reports of Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon and Iranian military action in northern Iraq, raising fears of a wider regional conflict. Stay with IndiaToday.in for latest and live updates-

How Bengal's Bapuji cake is still popular with GenZ and millennials alikeTrends may come and go, but Bapuji Cake's foundation remains unchanged. As the bakery's owner Amitava Jana said during an exclusive conversation, the beloved brand continues to be guided by three enduring values — humanity, loyalty and ethics.

UP woman befriends trader's son, shows Pak cleric's videos, converts himUP Police have arrested a physiotherapist and her father in connection with allegations that a Shamli businessman's son was coerced into changing his religion and identity as part of an alleged conspiracy to take control over the family's property.

At 26, man turned his IIT setback into a seven-figure salary and a BMW for dadAnkur Warikoo has recounted how Shaurya Shikhar went from having an IIT-JEE setback to buying his father a Rs 55 lakh BMW.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and the summer of suspended disbeliefHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the summer of 2026, a boy from Bihar walked to the crease and gave Indian cricket back something it had quietly lost: the pure, uncomplicated joy of watching without needing to explain why.

What triggered the 7.8 Philippines earthquake and why is the region so vulnerable?A 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck off Mindanao, setting off tsunami warnings and evacuations. The jolt has again highlighted the Philippines' exposure to powerful quakes along active plate boundaries.

Indian Railways to roll out upgraded reservation system from AugustIndian Railways will begin shifting trains to its upgraded Passenger Reservation System from August. The new platform brings better booking capacity, AI-powered waitlist predictions and a smoother ticket booking experience for passengers.

What's wrong with Aamir marrying at 60? Rakhee Gulzar questions ageist scrutinyRakhee Gulzar has welcomed Aamir Khan's decision to marry Gauri Spratt on July 5, 2026. She said love should not be defined by age.

DMK no longer an ally, TMC out of office: INDIA bloc faces toughest test todayLeaders of 23 opposition parties are set to meet in New Delhi today to review strategy, project unity and address differences. The gathering will test the INDIA bloc's cohesion as it plans for state contests and the 2029 Lok Sabha polls.

Video: Private jet crashes, bursts into flames during emergency landing; 2 deadA routine emergency landing attempt ended in tragedy when a private jet crashed near an international airport in the Dominican Republic. The pilot and co-pilot did not survive, raising questions about what went wrong.

Alexander Zverev leaves painful past behind, makes French Open his happy placeFrench Open 2026: Alexander Zverev has endured a difficult history at Roland Garros, including a serious injury in 2022 and a defeat in the 2024 final. But on Sunday, the German finally turned the page in Paris, capturing his first Grand Slam title and ending a long wait.

These Indian cities could see auroras as solar storm hits Earth tonightThe sky over India is about to do something extraordinary. A massive solar storm is hitting Earth tonight, and auroras, popularly known as northern lights, are closer to home than you ever imagined. Here is everything you need to know before midnight.

What constitutes a paper leak? Parliament panel asks NTA to reveal cases since 2018A parliamentary panel has asked the NTA to clarify what constitutes a paper leak, explain its handling of the NEET-UG controversy, and outline reforms introduced afterward. The panel is also reviewing CBSE's OSM tender process over concerns related to exam integrity and procurement practices.

WWDC 2026 could bring major changes for Apple facing its biggest test yetIndia Today is back in Cupertino a year after the iPhone launch. Here we find Apple preparing for a WWDC that could define its AI ambitions and the future of its software ecosystem.

El Nino effect: These cities will see dramatic changes in rain patternsA strong El Nino is building in the Pacific for June to August 2026, and the WMO expects it to reshape rainfall across the world's cities. From Delhi and Sydney to Johannesburg and the Ecuador coast, here is who faces drought and who faces downpours.

sports.headline: Flavio Cobolli follows Andreeva's example, thanks himself after French Open final French Open 2026: Flavio Cobolli thanked himself and praised Alexander Zverev after a five-set French Open final defeat. The loss ended his title bid but confirmed a breakthrough fortnight that changed his standing in the game.

In Roland Garros, Zverev finds freedom in maiden Grand Slam win Germany's Alexander Zverev finally won his maiden Grand Slam. Playing against Italy's Flavio Cobolli, Zverev finally found the freedom that he had been looking for.

Why Praggnanandhaa's win in Norway Chess 2026 brings back belief of good times in Indian Chess? Norway Chess 2026: Praggnanandhaa created history when he won the Norway Chess 2026 event in grand style. Not only did he become the first Indian to win the prestigious tournament, he also defeated Magnus Carlsen in his sentinel twice and that deserves a special mention in the annals of chess.

Only Test, Day 3 Live: India enforce follow-on after Manav Suthar's brilliance India (IND) vs Afghanistan (AFG) Only Test Live Score and Updates, Day 3: AFG (152-all-out) trail India by 412 runs. Manav Suthar's exceptional performance has helped India enforce follow-on, on Afghanistan. India are ahead by 412 runs and are expecte to seal the game by the end of the day.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and the summer of suspended disbelief In the summer of 2026, a boy from Bihar walked to the crease and gave Indian cricket back something it had quietly lost: the pure, uncomplicated joy of watching without needing to explain why.

Alexander Zverev leaves painful past behind, makes French Open his happy place French Open 2026: Alexander Zverev has endured a difficult history at Roland Garros, including a serious injury in 2022 and a defeat in the 2024 final. But on Sunday, the German finally turned the page in Paris, capturing his first Grand Slam title and ending a long wait.

From losers to Grand Slam champions: Zverev pays emotional tribute to his team Alexander Zverev finally ended his long pursuit of Grand Slam glory on Sunday, defeating Flavio Cobolli to win the French Open before thanking the team that stood beside him through injuries, heartbreak and repeated near-misses on tennis's biggest stages.

Neymar's last dance: Final shot at redemption or a farewell to Joga Bonito? FIFA World Cup 2026: With injuries recurring, Brazil's decision to bank on Neymar for what could be his final World Cup has reopened the old argument about promise, decline and necessity. Can Carlo Ancelotti's call turn a fading icon into the centre of Brazil's latest reckoning with hope and history?

Christian Eriksen collapses on pitch again, five years after cardiac arrest Christian Eriksen sparked fresh concern on Sunday after the Denmark midfielder collapsed on the pitch during an international friendly against Ukraine, forcing the match to be abandoned.

Watch: Zverev breaks down in tears after winning maiden Grand Slam at French Open Years of near-misses, injury setbacks and Grand Slam heartbreak finally gave way to tears of joy as Alexander Zverev defeated Flavio Cobolli on Sunday to win the French Open and claim the first Major title of his career.

Fourth time lucky: Alexander Zverev breaks Grand Slam curse with French Open title French Open 2026: Alexander Zverev had lost three Grand Slam finals earlier, but on Sunday, the German ended the streak with the first major of his career. On Sunday, the World No.3 took over four hours to beat Italy's Flavio Cobolli in a thrilling five-setter on Centre Court.

Pole vault king Mondo Duplantis suffers his first loss in nearly three yearsOlympic champion Armand 'Mondo' Duplantis suffered his first defeat in 35 months after Australia's Kurtis Marschall stunned the home crowd to win the pole vault competition at the Stockholm Diamond League.

Brazil call up Manchester United-linked Ederson after Wesley injuryBrazil have called up Atalanta midfielder Ederson to replace injured defender Wesley in their World Cup squad, with the late change offering Carlo Ancelotti an additional midfield option ahead of the tournament.

Kimi Antonelli makes it five wins in a row, becomes youngest to win Monaco GPKimi Antonelli became the youngest Monaco Grand Prix winner after overcoming a late red-flag restart to secure his fifth consecutive Formula One victory and extend his championship lead.

ENG vs NZ: Ben Stokes says Lord's pitch does little to help Test cricket's futureBen Stokes questioned the Lord's pitch after England's win over New Zealand, warning that extreme bowler-friendly surfaces could harm Test cricket's future and reduce the format's appeal.

Afghanistan coach rues costly DRS mistakes vs India: We were exceptionally rustyAfghanistan head coach Richard Pybus lamented his side's poor use of DRS, admitting they were "exceptionally rusty" and lacked the conviction needed to challenge key decisions.

Shreyas Iyer's sister pens heartfelt note after India T20I captaincy nodShresta Iyer shared an emotional Instagram post after Shreyas Iyer was named India's T20I captain. Her tribute underlined the setbacks, injuries and persistence behind his rise.

Manav Suthar's debut showed he can be a serious Test contender: Sunil GavaskarManav Suthar's impressive and accurate bowling on Test debut showed he has the potential to become a strong contender for a regular place in India's Test side, according to Sunil Gavaskar.

Manav Suthar's Test debut leaves father emotional: We were too nervous to watchManav Suthar's dream Test debut was a moment of immense pride for his family, but nerves and a touch of superstition meant they were not in the stands to witness the left-arm spinner's bowling heroics against Afghanistan in Mullanpur on Sunday.

Washington Sundar sees bright Test future for Manav SutharWashington Sundar praised debutant Manav Suthar after the left-arm spinner took three wickets against Afghanistan on day two in Mullanpur. Sundar said Suthar's skill, effort and attitude mark him out as a strong Test prospect for India.

Animesh Kujur breaches CWG 2026 qualifying standard, wins 200m silver in TaipeiNational record holder Animesh Kujur breached the qualifying standard for the 2026 Commonwealth Games after clinching silver in the men's 200m at the New Taipei City Athletics Open on Sunday.

Wish Bumrah committed himself for India: Ex-cricketer slams pick-and-choose approachSanjay Manjrekar criticized Jasprit Bumrah's on and off availability, questioning the premier pacer's commitment to the Indian national team after he was rested for India's upcoming T20I tour of the United Kingdom.

Pakistan Cricket Board mulls axing of Test coach Sarfaraz, captain Shan MasoodAfter Pakistan's 0-2 Test series loss to Bangladesh, the PCB is considering major changes to the team's set-up. Talks with Younis Khan and Mohammad Hafeez signal a wider overhaul, while head coach Sarfaraz Ahmed and captain Shan Masood are under pressure.

Comeback hero Robinson helps England crush New Zealand in Lord's rain-hit TestEngland sealed a commanding 115-run victory over New Zealand in a rain-affected first Test at Lord's on Sunday, taking a 1-0 lead in the three-match series as Ollie Robinson marked his return to Test cricket with a match-winning performance.

Who is Saleem Safi? Meet the first Afghan pacer to claim five wickets vs IndiaIND vs AFG: Once forced to nearly quit cricket due to severe financial hardships, Afghanistan's Mohammad Saleem Safi made history by taking a brilliant 6/140 against a powerful Indian batting lineup.

French Open Final Highlights: Zverev wins maiden Grand Slam in five-set thrillerFrench Open Men's Singles Final Highlights: Alexander Zverev's wait for a maiden Grand Slam has finally come to an end. The German managed to get past Italy's Flavio Cobolli in a hard-fought contest that went all the way up to five sets.

India firm grip on one-off Test in Mullanpur despite spirited Afghan fightbackIndia tightened their grip on the one-off Test against Afghanistan after posting 564/8 declared and reducing the visitors to a precarious position by stumps on Day 2. Debutant spinner Manav Suthar starred with the ball and picked up three wickets on Day 2.

Shreyas Iyer's bold first words as India captain: Won't be under anyone's shadowShreyas Iyer has embraced the challenge of becoming India's new T20I captain, but insists the leadership role will not change who he is. The Mumbai batter says he intends to stay true to his personality and lead on his own terms.

Group A preview: India face tricky test in three-way battleWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Group A features a high-stakes battle among six-time champions Australia, India and South Africa, alongside Pakistan, Bangladesh and debutants Netherlands.

Spinner Manav Suthar strikes in first over on Test debutIndia debutant Manav Suthar enjoyed a dream start to his Test career, claiming his maiden wicket in his very first over against Afghanistan. The left-arm spinner was India's standout bowler on Day 2 of the one-off Test in Mullanpur.

FIFA World Cup Group I Preview: Will Africa's elite outshine Mbappe vs HaalandGroup I preview: World Cup heavyweights France and a historic Senegal side collision in a fascinating section, while an explosive Norway team and stubborn Iraq look to cause ultimate chaos.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
